build.notes: Produces a single static binary at ~/Development/vgi-go/vgi-example-worker-go. Supports stdio, --http, and --unix (launcher) modes.

subprocess.notes: Windows: stdio subprocess transport over the built Go worker exe (launch:/unix: are POSIX-only). adapter.py appends .exe on Windows.

launch.notes: VGI launcher manages spawn + idle shutdown. Useful for cross-language fairness against Java (launcher there is required to avoid per-attach JVM startup).

launch-shm.notes: Same as launch but with shared-memory transport. Modest win at t=1 (+25%), bigger at t=4 (+42%), but Go's parallel scaling stays around 2.1× — arrow-go's per-batch allocations become the bottleneck once transport is removed.

notes: vgi-go is the Go port of the VGI protocol. Registers the `example` catalog with the same VGI function names as vgi-python and vgi-java (multiply, upper_case, sequence, echo, sum_all_columns, vgi_sum, vgi_avg, …).
